title: 'Dear Diana Hoff,' — abandoned letter opening, July 2021
html:
TL;DR

  • Two-line Apple Note from July 5, 2021 (Yahir age 18, ~7 weeks pre-OSU). Title: "Dear Diana Hoff,". Body is one line: "I want to preface by saying that I genuinely cherish all of the" — and stops mid-sentence. The note is a letter that never got finished.
  • Diana Hoff is the Renaissance/Ford-orbit administrator Yahir owes formal thanks to. The next day's note (2021 07 06 contact diana hoff summer todos) lists "-contact Diana Hoff" as a task — meaning the abandoned draft from July 5 became a to-do item by July 6 by getting downgraded from prose to checklist. A live specimen of the debt of gratitude friction loop.
  • Same architecture as the Aug-2018 water flow analogy seed — Yahir starts a deliberate piece of writing, the energy doesn't carry, and the unfinished form becomes the artifact. Cherish all of the — the missing object is what ought to be on the page.
  • Pairs as a triplet with the next day's Renaissance letter and the Aug 24 "Jann" people list — Yahir spends summer 2021 trying to compose formal thanks to the adults who funded his college path. The corpus shows him repeatedly opening these letters and rarely finishing them in note form.
  • The mid-sentence cut may also reflect that the actual letter got finished elsewhere (paper card, email, Google Doc) — the Apple Note is the workbench, not the deliverable. Worth grepping later notes for any "sent the Diana letter" closure.

Key quote

"I want to preface by saying that I genuinely cherish all of the —"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Documents the unfinished-letter sub-pattern within note as letter — when the addressee is real, the note's hardest move is the closing, not the opening.
  • Builds out the Diana Hoff page from a name-only stub toward a relationship arc.

Privacy

  • Diana Hoff is a public-facing scholarship administrator (already entitied). No new PII.

Wikilinks
